mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending expectations, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the industrial production index during late-cycle expansions reflect capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and force negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index values erode real purchasing power and force central banks to tighten policy, creating immediate downward pressure on equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases correlate with positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of monthly observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates structural labor market tightening, which raises wage costs and suppresses forward quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between PAYEMS and UNRATE shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, with rank correlation consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in real GDP during post-shock recovery phases trigger fiscal stimulus expectations and liquidity injections, which rapidly reverse bearish sentiment and generate positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 contractions in recovery regimes fail to produce positive 20-day returns, with mean forward returns remaining negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative decline in industrial production exceeding two percent signals severe supply-side disruption, prompting immediate risk-off positioning and negative weekly equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines more than 2 percent over 3 months
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of periods following a 3-month INDPRO decline exceeding 2 percent.
